question: Explain the molecular and cellular mechanisms by which CD4+ T‐helper 17 (Th17) cells differentiate from naïve CD4+ T cells and contribute to autoimmune tissue inflammation, with specific focus on the JAK-STAT–RORγt axis, IL-6–gp130 signaling, and the role of STAT3 and RORγt in chromatin remodeling of the Il17a/Il17f locus.
answer: Naïve CD4+ T cells integrate cytokine cues through Janus kinase–signal transducer and activator of transcription (JAK-STAT) pathways to adopt distinct effector lineages. Interleukin-6 (IL-6) binds its high-affinity receptor complex (IL-6Rα + gp130) on the T-cell surface, triggering gp130-associated JAK1/TYK2 phosphorylation. Activated JAK1 phosphorylates STAT3 at Tyr705; phosphorylated STAT3 dimerizes, translocates to the nucleus, and binds the Il17a/Il17f super-enhancer region, initiating permissive chromatin remodeling marked by H3K4me3 and H3K27ac deposition. Concurrently, retinoic-acid-receptor–related orphan receptor-γt (RORγt), the lineage-defining transcription factor, is upregulated via STAT3, forming a feed-forward circuit in which RORγt recruits the NCoA2 co-activator and the histone acetyltransferase p300 to consolidate Th17 identity. Mechanistically, RORγt displaces the repressive NR2F6-HDAC1 complex from the Il17a promoter while cooperating with BATF and IRF4 to recruit Mediator and cohesin at distal enhancer clusters (CNS-2, CNS-9), enabling long-range chromosomal interactions. Clinical relevance emerges in seronegative spondyloarthropathy and multiple sclerosis, where Th17-derived IL-17A/F drives neutrophil recruitment, MMP-3 secretion by synovial fibroblasts, and epithelial barrier disruption in the gut and synovium. Therapeutically, tofacitinib (JAK1/JAK3 inhibitor) reduces Th17 differentiation in psoriatic arthritis, and anti-IL-17 monoclonal antibodies (secukinumab, ixekizumab) block downstream signaling in plaque psoriasis. Single-cell ATAC-seq of synovial fluid from HLA-B27+ ankylosing spondylitis patients shows heightened STAT3-RORγt chromatin accessibility, a finding validated in the phase IIb TRANSFORM trial where JAK inhibition decreased Th17 frequency by 42%. In summary, the IL-6–gp130–JAK1–STAT3–RORγt axis provides a tractable mechanistic framework linking environmental cytokines to epigenetic remodeling and chronic inflammatory tissue damage, with direct translational implications for precision immunotherapy.
